title: UK Government Publishes Digital Inclusion Action Plan One-Year Progress Report
detail: The UK government published a one-year progress report on its Digital Inclusion Action Plan in April 2026, detailing plans to upgrade connectivity at 500+ community hubs in deprived areas before the end of 2026, complementing existing broadband subsidy and device access programs. The report highlighted progress on upskilling digitally excluded citizens, expanding affordable broadband options, and improving public access to online services. The UK approach — combining government commitments with partnerships with telecom providers, charities, and community organizations — is cited as a model for wealthy nations addressing domestic digital divides driven by cost, skills, and device barriers rather than pure infrastructure gaps.

sources.name: GOV.UK: Digital Inclusion Action Plan — One Year On
